RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Cross Defendant Richard Revel'S Demurrer To Clements' and Hanlin Cross-Complaint is overruled. Mr. Revel did not file a "demurrer" and the notice of demurrer refers to CCP 410.10 [jurisdiction] as the basis for the filing and that provision is inapposite. In addition, even if the court takes judicial notice of the deed and concludes that the 10/9/11 transfer date is not reasonably subject to dispute, the cross-complaints still state a claim. Reading the cross-complaints liberally per CCP 452, they both allege a basis for liability other than Mr. Revel's legal ownership of the property.
